result:
1. Trusts client `X-User-Id` header — handler reads `req.headers['x-user-id']` as identity. Section 2 rule 1 / Section 5: edge must never trust client-supplied identity headers; JWT verification sets `req.user` and forwards `X-Internal-User-Id` over mTLS. Fix: remove header read; use `req.user.sub` from `auth_jwt` middleware output. This is a P0 (auth bypass / impersonation).

2. No authentication or authorization on route — handler does not require `auth_jwt` to have run, and does not check that the caller belongs to `:org_id` or has a role allowed to move funds. Fix: ensure route is mounted after the pipeline's `auth_jwt`; assert `req.user.org === req.params.org_id` (or membership lookup) and that roles include a transfer-authorized role before calling `txSvc`.

3. No input validation on `from`, `to`, `amount`, `org_id` — body is destructured and forwarded raw to `tx-svc`. Fix: validate `org_id`/`from`/`to` as expected ID shapes, `amount` as positive integer (minor units) with an upper bound, reject mismatched currency / self-transfer; use a schema validator before dispatch. Section 12 priority 2.

4. No idempotency on a money-moving POST — repeated/retry requests will double-spend. Fix: require `Idempotency-Key` header, propagate to `tx-svc`, and have `tx-svc` dedupe. Transfers must be replay-safe at the API boundary independent of network retries.

5. Unhandled errors — `await txSvc.transfer(...)` has no try/catch and no `next(err)`. Rejections will bypass the central error handler in `src/edge/errorHandler.ts` and may leak stack/internal details or hang the response. Section 11 error-handling rule. Fix: wrap with try/catch and call `next(err)`, or use an `asyncHandler` wrapper; ensure response uses RFC 7807 shape.

6. Response shape violates RFC 7807 / missing `request_id` — `{ok: true}` on success is fine, but any error path must return `problem+json` with `request_id` matching the OTel trace (Section 2 rule 6). Currently there is no error path at all. Fix: on failure, return `{type,title,status,detail,instance,request_id}`; never surface `tx-svc` internals.

7. Route not registered through the pipeline — using `app.post(...)` at top level risks bypassing `header_sanitize`, `rate_limit`, `body_size_guard`, `auth_jwt`, `route_dispatcher`. Section 3 mandates ordered middleware. Fix: register under the router that sits after the full middleware chain in `src/edge/pipeline.ts`; do not attach money-moving routes directly to `app`.

8. No rate limit tuning for a sensitive write — default 30/min/IP for POST is too loose for fund transfers and too strict if a legitimate batch client retries. Fix: add a dedicated entry in `config/rate_limits.json` for `/v1/orgs/:org_id/transfer` (e.g. tighter per-user/per-org limit in addition to per-IP), and key on authenticated user, not just IP.

9. Direct call to `txSvc` bypasses `route_dispatcher` + `mTLS_egress` — handler appears to call a library directly instead of dispatching through the Envoy sidecar path (Section 3 steps 6–7). Fix: route through the standard dispatcher so mTLS, tracing, and `X-Internal-*` header propagation are applied; do not hand-roll egress.

10. Audit logging / PII risk — a money-movement action needs an audit log entry (actor, org, from, to, amount, request_id, jti) into the 365-day audit bucket (Section 9), and body/response must not be logged raw (Section 11). Fix: emit an explicit audit event via `audit_log` middleware or equivalent; ensure `pii_redact` covers any fields that may include account identifiers treated as PII.

11. `org_id` taken from path without cross-check against body/JWT — attacker can pass `org_id` in URL that differs from the account owners. Fix: verify `from`/`to` accounts both belong to `:org_id`, and `:org_id` matches `req.user.org` (or explicit membership), before dispatch. Prevents cross-tenant fund movement.

12. No body size / content-type guard assumption — relies on global `body_size_guard` (2MB) which is fine, but handler should reject non-`application/json` and unexpected fields. Fix: enforce `Content-Type: application/json` and strict schema (reject unknown keys) to avoid mass-assignment style issues being forwarded to `tx-svc`.
